rationale: Both evaluations give the identical score (4/10) and rest on the same substantive concerns — lack of direct experience in AI accelerators, SoC/IP modeling, and deep-learning performance analysis. The wording differs (e.g., 'diverse programming experience' vs 'strong programming skills', and the variant's third concern reframes the gap around the job's cloud/hardware focus), but these are reworded restatements of the same assessment, well within ordinary run-to-run noise. Nothing in the variant keys off the name change from Bogdan Szabo to Maria Rodriguez.
